medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ year old man who developed excruciating chest pain radiating to his back, which felt like a stabbing sensation. He presented to ___ and underwent CTA that revealed type A dissection. He was emergently transferred for surgical intervention and taken directly to the OR. Of note, he ran out of his blood pressure medication last week.

physical_examination.general_examination: Temp: 99, HR: 99, BP: 131/73, RR: 20, O2 sat: 96% 2 liters NC. RLE has VAC on lateral fasciotomy site, medial site closed with +drainage. Neuro: nonfocal except for RLE: no sensation below thigh.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Mild symmetric left ventricular hypertrophy with normal cavity size and global systolic function (LVEF>55%).

laboratory_examinations.findings: Glucose-109*, UreaN-42*, Creat-5.2*#, Na-127*, K-3.9, Cl-91*, HCO3-28, AnGap-12

radiographic_examinations.findings: Pre Bypass: left atrium elongated, mobile density seen in ascending aorta, aortic arch, and descending aorta consistent with intimal flap/aortic dissection; mild to moderate mitral regurgitation, trivial pericardial effusion. Post Bypass: sinus rhythm, aortic contours consistent with prosthetic graft, dissection flap still seen.
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: MR is now moderate; remaining exam unchanged.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Type A aortic dissection extends into the bilateral external iliac arteries; complete occlusion of the right external iliac artery; narrowing of the distal right popliteal artery possibly due to thrombosis; new pericardial fluid with associated enhancement representing pericarditis.
Arterial and venous flow demonstrated within the right kidney without evidence of renal artery stenosis; left main renal artery appears patent with delayed acceleration time.
Acute occlusive thrombus seen within the left cephalic vein; no DVT visualized in either arm.
No evidence of deep venous thrombosis in the right or left lower extremity veins.
No gallstones or evidence of acute cholecystitis; borderline splenomegaly.
Type A aortic dissection extending from ascending aorta post stent to bilateral external iliac arteries, moderate segment of high-grade stenosis in left external iliac artery, new pericardial fluid.
Lumbosacral nerve roots symmetric with normal signal intensity and no evidence of edema.

other_tests.findings: Mild retrocardiac atelectasis; no evidence of pleural effusions.
